title: Search filter inverted: 'active tasks' filter returns archived tasks and vice versa since v2.3.1
problem: The search filter logic on the main dashboard search bar is inverted. When the default 'active tasks only' filter is applied, search results return archived tasks instead of active ones. Conversely, manually selecting 'show archived' displays active tasks. This affects all users and all searches, not just specific keywords.
root_cause_hypothesis: The v2.3.1 patch (deployed ~3 days ago) likely introduced a regression that inverted the boolean logic or enum mapping for the search filter's active/archived status. This is server-side since it reproduces across different browsers and operating systems.
reproduction_steps: Log into TaskFlow (v2.3.1) on the main dashboard
Use the main search bar at the top of the dashboard
Type any keyword (e.g., 'Q2 planning') and press Enter with the default 'active tasks only' filter
Observe that results contain archived tasks rather than active ones
Manually switch the filter to 'show archived'
Observe that active tasks now appear in the results instead of archived ones
environment: TaskFlow v2.3.1 (patched ~3 days ago). Reproduced on Chrome ~124-125 / Windows 11 and Firefox / macOS. Server-side issue — browser and OS independent.
impact: All users performing searches are seeing inverted results. Active tasks are effectively hidden by default, making search unreliable for day-to-day task management. Multiple users confirmed affected.
recommended_fix: Investigate the v2.3.1 patch for changes to search filter logic. Look for an inverted boolean condition, swapped enum values, or a negation error in the query that maps the active/archived filter parameter to the database query. Check the search API endpoint's filter handling and compare against the v2.3.0 behavior.
proposed_test_case: Create one active task and one archived task with the same keyword. Search with the 'active only' filter and assert the active task is returned and the archived task is not. Then search with the 'archived' filter and assert the inverse. Regression test should cover both filter directions.
information_gaps: Exact commit or changelog for the v2.3.1 patch that may have introduced the regression
Whether the issue also affects API-based searches or only the dashboard UI search
